message:
fix: harden extension popularity, file tools, and JS-to-JSON conversion

Three independent safety/security fixes:

1. extension_popularity: reject non-GitHub SCP-style URLs in
   github_repo_candidate_from_url(). Previously any git@host:path URL
   was accepted as a GitHub repo since the host portion was never
   checked, misclassifying GitLab/Bitbucket SSH remotes. Add a host
   guard for github.com on the SCP path and a regression test.

2. tools: guard ReadTool, EditTool, and HashlineEditTool against
   non-regular-file paths (directories, device files, etc.) by adding
   an is_file() check after fs::metadata(). Previously these would pass
   the metadata check and fail with confusing errors downstream.

3. extensions_js: cap JS-to-JSON object property iteration at 100,000
   in js_to_json_inner() to prevent unbounded memory allocation from
   malicious or buggy extensions returning objects with millions of
   properties.

Also suppress a clippy false positive on drain_bash_output's
&mut Receiver parameter (needed for Send bound on async future).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
